heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 106–224, title IV, § 438(a)(4)114 Stat. 454June 20, 2000
label: 2801 to 2813 Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 106–224, title IV, § 438(a)(4)114 Stat. 454June 20, 2000
notes: Section 2801, , , , set forth congressional findings regarding importation or distribution in interstate commerce of noxious weeds.Pub. L. 93–629, § 288 Stat. 2148Jan. 3, 1975
Section 2802, , , ; , , , defined terms as used in this chapter.Pub. L. 93–629, § 388 Stat. 2148Pub. L. 105–86, title VII, § 728111 Stat. 2108Jan. 3, 1975Nov. 18, 1997

Section 2803, , , ; , , ; , , , prohibited movement of noxious weeds into or through the United States or interstate, unless in accordance with regulations to prevent dissemination.Pub. L. 93–629, § 488 Stat. 2149Pub. L. 100–449, title III, § 301(f)(4)102 Stat. 1869Pub. L. 103–465, title IV, § 431(f)108 Stat. 4968Jan. 3, 1975Sept. 28, 1988Dec. 8, 1994
Section 2804, , , , authorized promulgation of quarantines and other regulations requiring inspection of articles and means of conveyance.Pub. L. 93–629, § 588 Stat. 2149Jan. 3, 1975

Section 2805, , , , authorized Secretary of Agriculture to seize, quarantine, treat, destroy, or otherwise dispose of infested articles or means of conveyance.Pub. L. 93–629, § 688 Stat. 2149Jan. 3, 1975
Section 2806, , , , authorized warrantless inspections of articles and means of conveyance and searches of premises pursuant to warrants.Pub. L. 93–629, § 788 Stat. 2150Jan. 3, 1975
Section 2807, , , , set forth penalties for violations of sections 2803, 2804, and regulations promulgated under this chapter.Pub. L. 93–629, § 888 Stat. 2151Jan. 3, 1975

Section 2808, , , , related to cooperation with Federal, State, and local agencies and appointment of employees thereof as collaborators to assist in administration of provisions of this chapter.Pub. L. 93–629, § 988 Stat. 2151Jan. 3, 1975
Section 2809, , , , authorized promulgation of regulations necessary to effectuate provisions of this chapter.Pub. L. 93–629, § 1088 Stat. 2151Jan. 3, 1975
Section 2810, , , , authorized appropriations and described availability of sums and limitations on their use.Pub. L. 93–629, § 1188 Stat. 2151Jan. 3, 1975
Section 2811, , , , related to inapplicability of provisions of this chapter to shipments of seed subject to certain other laws.Pub. L. 93–629, § 1288 Stat. 2152Jan. 3, 1975

Section 2812, , , , provided that provisions of this chapter would not invalidate provisions of State and local laws, except as such laws would permit prohibited actions.Pub. L. 93–629, § 1388 Stat. 2152Jan. 3, 1975
Section 2813, , , , related to separability of provisions.Pub. L. 93–629, § 1488 Stat. 2152Jan. 3, 1975
Short Title, , , provided:Pub. L. 93–629, § 188 Stat. 2148Jan. 3, 1975“That this Act [enacting this chapter] may be cited as the ‘Federal Noxious Weed Act of 1974’.”
